purpose: Contract-based parameters for filling each promoted lecture top tab from ABPath AP content-specification anchors while preserving content-area scope.

globalParameters.conformanceTarget: L4: topic mapped, level declared, and cross-links to lecture tab outputs present
globalParameters.scopeRule: Each lecture is governed by its content area. ABPath topics outside the scoped content area may be used only for mimic, staging, specimen-handling, or quality/safety contrast.

lectures.title: Renal Mass Evaluation
lectures.scopeBoundary: Kidney/renal pathology and renal specimen handling only; bladder topics may support urothelial differential context but must not become the main lecture.
contractWarnings: The normalized AP seed currently has stronger medical-kidney/process anchors than renal neoplasm anchors; renal tumor-specific anchors should be added when the source specification is expanded.

abpathAnchors.title: Tissue processing of renal biopsies
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > Tissue processing of renal biopsies

abpathAnchors.title: End Stage Renal Disease
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > End Stage Renal Disease

abpathAnchors.title: Monoclonal gammopathy associated renal disease
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > Monoclonal gammopathy associated renal disease

abpathAnchors.title: Renal transplantation, donor assessment
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > Renal transplantation, donor assessment

abpathAnchors.title: Diseases associated with acellular closure of glomerular capillaries
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > Diseases associated with acellular closure of glomerular capillaries

abpathAnchors.title: Non-proliferative glomerulonephropathies
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > Non-proliferative glomerulonephropathies

abpathAnchors.title: Pyelonephritis
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > Pyelonephritis

abpathAnchors.title: Tubulointerstitial Disease
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > Tubulointerstitial Disease

abpathAnchors.title: Vascular lesions
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > Vascular lesions

abpathAnchors.title: Acute diffuse intracapillary proliferative glomerulonephritis
abpathAnchors.path: The Genitourinary System > Kidney > Medical Kidney Disease > Acute diffuse intracapillary proliferative glomerulonephritis

required: State that Genitourinary: kidney/renal pathology evaluation is the scoped content area.
List at least five ABPath-derived objectives using Tissue processing of renal biopsies and related anchors.
Show a faculty run sheet that starts with the ABPath content-specification frame.
overview.acceptance: Learner can say what ABPath domain is being taught and what report-level task the session prepares them to perform.

required: Start from specimen type, clinical question, site, and adequacy.
Commit to low-power architecture before high-power details.
Use Tissue processing of renal biopsies versus End Stage Renal Disease, Monoclonal gammopathy associated renal disease, Renal transplantation, donor assessment as the core differential frame.
Select ancillary studies only when they change classification, staging, margin, adequacy, or management language.
diagnosticApproach.acceptance: Algorithm endpoint produces a defensible diagnosis/report consequence rather than a memorized entity label.

required: Begin with normal histology or expected tissue compartment for the content area.
Require low-power pattern recognition, high-power discriminator, closest mimic, and report consequence.
Use image assets only when source/provenance and faculty review are available; otherwise show the checklist scaffold.
microscopy.acceptance: Microscopy tab teaches an inspection sequence even before final licensed images are attached.

required: Provide entity cards for the highest-yield ABPath anchors in this content area.
For each entity, include key morphology, targeted ancillary logic, critical differential, and management/reporting implication.
Keep criteria tied to the scoped content area; do not promote unrelated organ-system material.
diagnosticCriteria.acceptance: Diagnostic criteria are actionable at sign-out and mapped to the lecture ABPath anchors.

required: Include oral recall/retrieval prompts before answer reveal.
Include at least two MCQs that test workflow and reporting consequence.
Include flashcards for spaced recall of content-area anchors and diagnostic sequence.
questions.acceptance: Assessment checks recognition, differential logic, ancillary appropriateness, and report consequence.

required: Preserve the source lecture transcript/body as provenance.
Do not silently replace source content with generated contract text.
Use the generated ABPath parameters as scaffolding around the transcript.
fullText.acceptance: Transcript remains available as source material while tabs provide structured ABPath learning tasks.

lectures.exclusionRule: A lecture may reference adjacent content only as a brief mimic, staging, or specimen-handling contrast; it should not add objectives, MCQs, or entity cards outside its content-area scope.
lectures.promotionGate: Promote only when all six top tabs render, the content-area boundary is explicit, ABPath anchors are visible, and learner assessment ties back to report-level consequence.

required: State that Genitourinary: renal/urologic oncology overview is the scoped content area.
List at least five ABPath-derived objectives using Tissue processing of renal biopsies and related anchors.
Show a faculty run sheet that starts with the ABPath content-specification frame.
overview.acceptance: Learner can say what ABPath domain is being taught and what report-level task the session prepares them to perform.

required: Start from specimen type, clinical question, site, and adequacy.
Commit to low-power architecture before high-power details.
Use Tissue processing of renal biopsies versus BCG effects, Diverticula, End Stage Renal Disease as the core differential frame.
Select ancillary studies only when they change classification, staging, margin, adequacy, or management language.
diagnosticApproach.acceptance: Algorithm endpoint produces a defensible diagnosis/report consequence rather than a memorized entity label.

required: Begin with normal histology or expected tissue compartment for the content area.
Require low-power pattern recognition, high-power discriminator, closest mimic, and report consequence.
Use image assets only when source/provenance and faculty review are available; otherwise show the checklist scaffold.
microscopy.acceptance: Microscopy tab teaches an inspection sequence even before final licensed images are attached.

required: Provide entity cards for the highest-yield ABPath anchors in this content area.
For each entity, include key morphology, targeted ancillary logic, critical differential, and management/reporting implication.
Keep criteria tied to the scoped content area; do not promote unrelated organ-system material.
diagnosticCriteria.acceptance: Diagnostic criteria are actionable at sign-out and mapped to the lecture ABPath anchors.

required: Include oral recall/retrieval prompts before answer reveal.
Include at least two MCQs that test workflow and reporting consequence.
Include flashcards for spaced recall of content-area anchors and diagnostic sequence.
questions.acceptance: Assessment checks recognition, differential logic, ancillary appropriateness, and report consequence.

required: Preserve the source lecture transcript/body as provenance.
Do not silently replace source content with generated contract text.
Use the generated ABPath parameters as scaffolding around the transcript.
fullText.acceptance: Transcript remains available as source material while tabs provide structured ABPath learning tasks.

lectures.exclusionRule: A lecture may reference adjacent content only as a brief mimic, staging, or specimen-handling contrast; it should not add objectives, MCQs, or entity cards outside its content-area scope.
lectures.promotionGate: Promote only when all six top tabs render, the content-area boundary is explicit, ABPath anchors are visible, and learner assessment ties back to report-level consequence.
